QUICK LINKS Grievance Redressal Committee Anti Ragging Committee Student Welfare Counsellor Committee Anti Ragging Committee SC/ST Cell Contact STUDY IN INDIA X TNEACODE 2739 Campus tour Admission 2025 TNEACODE 2739 MECHANICAL ENGINEERING 0 LPA HIGHEST SALARY PACKAGE 0 + RECRUITERS 0 + PUBLICATIONS 0 + PATENTS 10LPA HIGHEST SALARY PACKAGE 80+ RECRUITERS 220+ PUBLICATIONS 40+ PATENTS PROGRAM SCOPE A mechanical engineering program offers scopes across various industries and sectors

. Students who successfully completes the Mechanical engineering program can design and manufacture vehicles including electric vehicle, engines, various automotive systems, aircraft, spacecraft, missiles, solar systems, wind turbines, hydroelectric power systems, medical devices, prosthetics and biomedical equipment. Graduates can also design and develop robotic systems, automation systems and implement AI/ML techniques in this field

. Graduates can also design and develop robotic systems, automation systems and implement AI/ML techniques in this field. This program also paves the way to research and development activities across various industries, exploring new materials, technologies, and processes to drive innovation and improve performance and provides solid foundation for becoming consultant and entrepreneur Eventually, the scope of a mechanical engineering program is vast and diverse, offering numerous opportunities for graduates to pursue rewarding careers in various industries and make meaningful contributions to society through innovation and problem-solving

. LEARNING SCOPE Mechanical Engineering provides a solid foundation in engineering principles and prepares students for a dynamic and rewarding career in various industries. Students will learn fundamental principles of materials, mathematics, and mechanics that form the basis of mechanical engineering. This includes topics such as statics, dynamics, thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, manufacturing, and mechanical design. Students can design, analyse, and optimize mechanical systems and components by the use of Computer-Aided Design (CAD), Finite Element Analysis (FEA), and Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) knowledge

. Other important scope of this program is additive manufacturing (3D printing), quality control, process optimization, robotic and industrial automation, control systems, mechatronics, renewable energy systems, HVAC (Heating, Ventilation, And Air Conditioning), green technologies, vehicle dynamics, propulsion systems, aerodynamics and Applications of Artificial Intelligence, Machine learning, Data science and Industrial IOT. CAREER PROGRESSION With the advent of Nanotechnology, Acoustical Engineering and Additive Technology, the scope of Mechanical Engineering has expanded rapidly. There are limitless opportunities for the freshly minted Mechanical Engineers, whether they want to be employed or become an entrepreneur or pursue higher studies

. The Students have huge scope for employement as Design Engineer, R&D Engineer, ERP Consultant, Process Engineer, Systems Engineer, Sales Engineer, Product Quality Engineer, Test Engineer and Project Engineer. VISION To become an integrated center of excellence focusing on design, manufacturing and industrial engineering, providing societal benefits through academic services including research and innovation. MISSION Develop curriculum and delivery approach ensuing horizontal exposure and vertical expertise to the learners. Provide opportunities for faculties to enrich their knowledge and skills. Establish connections for local, national and global expertise for an active enhanced learning environment

. Establish connections for local, national and global expertise for an active enhanced learning environment. Execute societal outreach activity providing solutions to industries and society Establish contemporary facilities for providing practical learning experience. PROGRAM DETAILS Program Educational Objectives PEO1: Graduates will take up careers in Mechanical Engineering fields like Manufacturing, Design, Thermal and involve in carrying out mechanical engineering products. PEO2: Graduates will engage in a post graduate program in the field of Engineering Design leading to academic and research careers. PEO3: Graduates will take up entrepreneurship as a career

. PEO3: Graduates will take up entrepreneurship as a career. Program Outcomes O1: Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization to develop the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2: Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development. PO3: Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society, and environment as required

. PO4: Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis & interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions. PO5: Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems. PO6: The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture, and environment

. PO7: Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws. PO8: Individual and Collaborative Teamwork: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams. PO9: Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences

. PO10: Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments. PO11: Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. Program Specific Outcomes PSO1: Apply contemporary approaches in the field of manufacturing and design. PSO2: Apply concepts and approaches for improving the productivity in a professional working environment

. PSO2: Apply concepts and approaches for improving the productivity in a professional working environment. . All Rights Reserved. Dr.T.Ramakrishnan M.E., PhD Areas of Specialization Bio-Composites, Composite Materials, Polymer Matrix Composites, Natural Fiber Reinforced Composites. Dr. Ramakrishnan holds a PhD in Polymer composites from Anna University, Chennai. He is recognized as an Anna University Supervisor and presently guiding 6 research scholars. Actively involved as Co-PI in Advanced Material Processing and Testing Research Center funded by Department of Science and Technology under FIST scheme. He has 20 + quality publications to his credit and is an active editor and reviewer in reputed journals. He has received the Best Researcher Award and recognition for the best paper from Sri Eshwar College of Engineering for two consecutive years

. He has received the Best Researcher Award and recognition for the best paper from Sri Eshwar College of Engineering for two consecutive years. Recently, he received the Top 5% Researchers Award and the Best Researchers Award in SECE during the Annual Day Function 2024. Dr.S.Venkatesh M.E., PhD., PDF D.O.J : 18.06.2014 Areas of Specialization Particle Technology, CFD, Mulitphase Flow, Turbomachinery, Optimization, Emission Control Equipment Design. Dr. Venkatesh is a Post Doctorate with specialization in Particle Technology and Computational Fluid Dynamics. He is recognized as an Anna University supervisor guiding four research scholars. Recently he received a grant of 18.3 lakh grant from the SERB-TARE scheme and is a Co-PI in DST FIST funding of 59 Lakhs

. Recently he received a grant of 18.3 lakh grant from the SERB-TARE scheme and is a Co-PI in DST FIST funding of 59 Lakhs. He has published more than 27 articles in SCI and Scopus-indexed journals and published two books. He is also an active editor and reviewer in many reputed SCI journals. He has received an outstanding reviewer award from Journal of Aerosol Science and an recipient of top 5% researcher award from Sri Eshwar College of Engineering. Dr.N.K.Millerjothi M.E., PhD D.O.J : 04.04.2022 Areas of Specialization Thermo-fluid Engineering, Energy Conservation Audit, Energy Management, Renewable Energy systems, Alternative fuels Dr. Millerjothi holds a PhD in alternative fuels and is an Anna University recognized supervisor guiding one scholar

. Millerjothi holds a PhD in alternative fuels and is an Anna University recognized supervisor guiding one scholar. He has 25 years of academic experience in Mechanical Engineering, and 6 years of industrial experience both in India and abroad. He is the Principal Investigator for energy consultancy projects in around 35 industries. He has 40 research articles in peer-reviewed journals to his credit and has 1 patent granted and 5 patents published. He has received research funding from Germany worth $10,500 and CSIR funding for organizing international conferences. He has participated in over 15 national and international seminars, attended around 25 workshops,12 training programs and served as a peer reviewer for over 5 international conferences

. He has also evaluated more than 8 PhD theses as a foreign examiner and 3 PhD theses as an Indian examiner. He holds membership to 6 professional bodies in India and abroad. Dr.P.Ganeshan M.E., PhD., M.B.A D.O.J : 10.12.2021 Areas of Specialization Composite Material for Engineering Applications, Additive Manufacturing Dr. Ganeshan holds a PhD in Mechanical Engineering and is an Anna University recognized supervisor guiding 10 scholars. He has more than 15.6 years of experience in teaching, research, and industry. He has published 102 research articles in peer reviewed Journals and has 10 patents published with 3 patents granted. He has participated in 12 International and National conferences. He is an active member in various professional bodies

. He has participated in 12 International and National conferences. He is an active member in various professional bodies. He is the recipient of Innovative Professional Award from Society of Professional Engineers India during the year 2016. He has published 21 papers in Scopus and SCI journals. He is also a member of ISTE, IWS and IE (India) and special invitee of Coimbatore Productivity Council (CPC)

. He is also a member of ISTE, IWS and IE (India) and special invitee of Coimbatore Productivity Council (CPC). He has also authored a textbook on Engineering Graphics. Tech in Mining & Machinery Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad and B

. He completed his M. Tech in Mining & Machinery Engineering from IIT (ISM) Dhanbad and B. Tech in Mechanical Engineering from Sant Gadge Baba Amravati University Maharashtra. He has 5 years of research experience at CSIR-Central Mechanical Research Institute. He was a recipient of the CSIR-SRF Scholarship for Doctoral Study and presently he is one of the Co-PI in DST FIST funding of 59 Lakhs. He is also an expert in CFD analysis and optimization. He also spearheads consultancy activities in design and New Product Development NPD.
